titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Bateman Family Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[BATEMAN FAMILY PAPERS ])//EN" "bateman.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence between family members. Includes letters between Rebekah Clegg Bateman and her husband, Thomas Bateman, her son, William Bateman, her father, Arthur Clegg, and her sister, Elizabeth Clegg Wilson; letters between Rebekah Bateman and her brothers, William and Thomas Bateman; one letter from Thomas Bateman to his son, Thomas; and letters between William Bateman and his brother, Thomas, and his father, Thomas. Many of the letters are written by and to children at school. Also included are hymns, poetry, prayers, and excerpts from sermons written down by Rebekah Clegg Bateman; and a travel diary written by her son, Thomas, about a visit to the Lake Country.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased in 1974 from Myers, Ltd. for the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Bateman Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bateman Family Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: BATEMAN FAMILY CHART
bioghist.p: NB: Limited to persons represented in the Bateman Family Papers.

bioghist.head: WILLIAM BATEMAN (1787-1835)
bioghist.p: William Bateman was the eldest son of Thomas Bateman, a Dissenting Manchester cotton merchant, and his wife Rebekah Clegg Bateman. He was apprenticed to and later joined his father in the cotton business, but by the early 1820s he retired to Middleton by Youlgrave in Derbyshire. A Fellow of the Society of Antiquaries, Bateman undertook several excavations of barrows and tumuli in the Peak district, publishing notes on these in the Archaeologia. Some of his dig notes were arranged and published by his son Thomas, who was also an amateur archaeologist, in C. R. Smith's Collectanea Antiqua . Bateman died on June 11, 1835.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
The Bateman Family
Papers
consist of correspondence and related material connected to the family of Thomas Bateman of Manchester. The papers span the dates 1756-1818, and have been arranged alphabetically by author. They were apparently selected, bound, and annotated by William Bateman (1787-1835) around 1818, and later partially disbound. Papers relating to his brother Thomas Bateman, Jr., who died of "water on the brain" in 1810 at the age of 18, had been previously gathered and annotated by Thomas Bateman (ca. 1765-1847).

Thomas Bateman and his wife, Rebekah Clegg Bateman, were fervent Dissenters, and their correspondence both with each other and with other family members reflects their strong interest in religious matters. Many letters contain reports of sermons, prayer sessions, meditations on Bible verses, self-examinations and exhortations to lead a Christian life. Other topics include family news, discussions of health and schooling for the children, arrangements for travel and visits, and errands to be done while Thomas is in London or Manchester on business.

The letters of Elizabeth Clegg Wilson to her sister Rebekah are particularly strong in reports of church and chapel events; news of neighbors, fellow church members, and distant relatives; and analyses of her personal spiritual state. Elizabeth was particularly concerned with the quality of local ministers, and they did not all meet with her approval; in 1796 she noted of "Mr. Lindal" that "I wish he would give up preaching and go to his weaving or whatever was his trade."

Rebekah Clegg Bateman's own religious concerns are documented in the collection of her miscellaneous papers located in Box 2, folder 12. These include her "Parts" for Sabbath Day exercises; copies of prayers and hymns; and inspirational quotations from sermons.

The letters exchanged among the younger Bateman and Wilson cousins contain school and family news, humorous comments on presents and upcoming family visits, and occasional mentions of parental finances and business conditions. Thomas Bateman's 1809 letters to his brother William describe his walking tour of the Lake District and his reaction to a Fourth of July celebration by American sailors in Liverpool which was disrupted by British carpenters. Further description of his Lake District tour, as well as other personal papers, a pencil portrait of him, and accounts of his illness and death, can be be found in folders 16-18. A copy of his funeral sermon, delivered by the Reverend Peter Samuel Charrier, is located in folder 22.

did.unittitle: 10 ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; various places
2 ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; Chester
8 ALS to Thomas Bateman, Jr.; various places

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Most of these letters also include
notes to Thomas from cousin Rebecca
Wilson.

did.unittitle: 11 ALS and 1 AL to William Bateman; Islington and other places
47 ALS to her husband Thomas Bateman; mostly from Manchester
2 ALS to her son William Bateman; Gatley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Accompanied by copy of the 1797
letter in William Bateman's hand,
poem on "happy scenes of youth"
dated "May 1818", and notes on
his mother's death.

did.unittitle: ALS to her father Arthur Clegg; Gatley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
With 2 copies, annotated with
information on "her sudden death
at Gatley Hall"

did.unittitle: 4 ALS to Margery Smithson; various places
20 ALS to Mary Jane Hodson Durdon Barclay Whitby; Manchester, etc.
3 ALS to her sister Elizabeth Clegg Wilson; various places

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Includes devotional poetry, prayers,
school exercises, excerpts from
sermons, hymns, accounts,
and medical prescriptions for
William Bateman.

did.unittitle: 5 ALS to his son Thomas Bateman, Jr.; various places
ALS (draft?) to his sister Rebekah Bateman; Liverpool
16 ALS to his brother William Bateman; various places

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Accompanied by notes, a poem, and
a pencil portrait of Thomas by
William Bateman.

did.unittitle: MS account of the final illness and death of Thomas Bateman, Jr, with notes on his "delirious conversation" and medical treatment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Apprenticing Thomas Bateman, Jr. to
his father Thomas for seven years,
to be taught "the business of a
cotton merchant."

did.unittitle: "Letters belonging to my late dear boy Thomas, found in his drawers May 1810"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Letters and papers describing his
1809 tour of the Lake District;
Bound with poems, school exercises,
cash accounts, and other papers,
and annotated by Thomas Bateman.

did.unittitle: ALS to (his father) Thomas Bateman; Pauls Cray [Kent]
4 ALS to (his brother) Thomas Bateman, Jr.; Manchester
Funeral sermon on the death of Thomas Bateman (1791-1810), holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Sermon text is Psa. 102:23.
Annotated by William Bateman.

did.unittitle: ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman and Elizabeth Clegg Wilson; Manchester
4 ALS to her daughter Rebekah Clegg Bateman; various places
5 ALS to her daughters Rebekah Clegg Bateman and Elizabeth Clegg Wilson; Manchester
ALS to Thomas Bateman, Jr.; Manchester

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Letter of condolence on the death
of Rebekah Clegg Bateman.

did.unittitle: ALS to the Rev. Mr. [?]; Stockport

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Sending books to be studied by
Clegg's daughters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Docketed: "A letter from one of the
Missionarys gone to South Sea
Islands...." Religious sentiments.

did.unittitle: ALS to Elizabeth Clegg Wilson; Bolton
ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; Mile End
WHITBY, MARY JANE HODSON DURDON BARCLAY
3 ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; Chester
ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; Manchester
2 ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; Manchester
46 ALS to her sister Rebekah Clegg Bateman; various places

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Letterbook. Family news, religious
discussion, comments on the Dissenting
church at Manchester and elsewhere.
Bound with printed portraits of
several ministers mentioned in
the letters.

did.unittitle: 4 ALS to her brother-in-law Thomas Bateman, London
2 ALS to Rebekah Clegg Bateman; Highbury Place
ALS to Thomas Bateman; Tyndale Place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Condolence letter on the death
of Rebekah Clegg Bateman.
